name: Boss SD-1 Super Overdrive
description: The Boss SD-1 is one of the most iconic overdrive pedals ever made. Its asymmetric clipping circuit delivers a warm, natural overdrive that responds beautifully to your playing dynamics.
pros: Great for stacking with other pedals
cons: Can sound harsh at high gain settings
No LED indicator on older models

name: Ibanez TS9 Tube Screamer
description: The Tube Screamer is the overdrive pedal that defined a genre. Its mid-focused tone cuts through any mix and pairs perfectly with a cranked tube amp.

name: Boss DS-1 Distortion
description: The Boss DS-1 has been the go-to distortion pedal since 1978. Used by Kurt Cobain, Steve Vai, and countless others, it delivers everything from mild grit to full-on distortion.
cons: Can sound fizzy at extreme settings

name: ProCo RAT2 Distortion
description: The RAT is a desert-island distortion pedal. Its unique filter control lets you shape the tone from bright and cutting to dark and fuzzy, covering an enormous range of gain textures.
pros: Works as overdrive, distortion, or fuzz

name: Electro-Harmonix Big Muff Pi
description: The Big Muff Pi is the sound of grunge, shoegaze, and psychedelic rock. Its massive, sustaining fuzz tone has been a staple on pedalboards since the 1970s.
cons: Can get lost in a band mix

name: Dunlop FFM3 Jimi Hendrix Fuzz Face Mini
description: The Fuzz Face is where it all started. This mini version captures the classic silicon Fuzz Face tone that Jimi Hendrix made legendary, in a pedalboard-friendly size.
pros: Cleans up well with volume knob

name: Boss DD-8 Digital Delay
description: The Boss DD-8 packs 11 delay modes into a compact pedal. From pristine digital repeats to warm analog-style echoes and shimmer, it covers every delay sound you could need.

name: MXR M169 Carbon Copy Analog Delay
description: The Carbon Copy is the gold standard of analog delay. Its warm, dark repeats add depth without cluttering your tone, making it perfect for any genre.

name: Boss CS-3 Compression Sustainer
description: The Boss CS-3 is a studio-quality compressor in a pedal. It evens out your dynamics, adds sustain, and gives your clean tone that polished, professional feel.
cons: Can add noise at high settings

name: Keeley Compressor Plus
description: The Keeley Compressor Plus is a boutique-quality compressor that adds sustain and sparkle without squashing your dynamics. The blend knob lets you mix compressed and dry signal for transparent compression.

name: Boss CE-5 Chorus Ensemble
description: The Boss CE-5 delivers lush, shimmering chorus with high and low filter controls that let you shape the effect precisely. From subtle shimmer to deep warble, it does it all.
cons: Digital sound may not suit purists
Can sound thin at extreme settings

name: Walrus Audio Julia V2 Chorus/Vibrato
description: The Julia V2 is a modern classic. Its lag control adds a unique character to the chorus effect, and the dry/wet blend lets you dial in everything from subtle shimmer to seasick vibrato.
cons: Lag control takes time to understand

name: Boss RC-5 Loop Station
description: The Boss RC-5 is a powerful looper in a compact pedal. With 13 hours of recording time, 99 phrase memories, and built-in drum rhythms, it is perfect for practice and performance.

name: TC Electronic Ditto Looper
description: The Ditto is the simplest looper you can buy. One knob, one switch, 5 minutes of loop time. It does one thing and does it perfectly — no menus, no complications.

name: Boss TU-3 Chromatic Tuner
description: The Boss TU-3 is the industry standard tuner pedal. Bright LED display visible in any lighting, accurate to +/- 1 cent, and it mutes your signal while tuning.
pros: Powers other pedals via daisy chain

name: MXR Phase 90
description: The MXR Phase 90 is the phaser that defined the effect. One knob controls the speed, and the lush, swirling tone adds movement to clean and dirty tones alike. Used by Van Halen and countless others.
cons: Can be too intense for some

name: Boss TR-2 Tremolo
description: The Boss TR-2 delivers classic tremolo from subtle pulse to choppy stutter. The wave control lets you shape the tremolo from smooth sine wave to hard square wave.

name: Boss GE-7 Graphic Equalizer
description: The Boss GE-7 gives you surgical control over your tone with 7 frequency bands. Use it to shape your amp tone, boost for solos, or tame problem frequencies in your signal chain.
pros: Can be used as a clean boost

name: Electro-Harmonix Small Clone
description: The Small Clone is the chorus pedal that Kurt Cobain made famous. Its lush, watery chorus tone is instantly recognizable and adds a haunting depth to clean tones.

name: Boss MT-2 Metal Zone
description: The Metal Zone is the most divisive pedal ever made — and one of the best-selling. Its parametric EQ and extreme gain make it a metal machine when dialed in correctly.
cons: Easy to dial in bad tones

name: Electro-Harmonix Soul Food
description: Klon Centaur clone at a fraction of the price. Transparent overdrive with touch-sensitive dynamics that clean up beautifully with your guitar's volume knob.
cons: Not as refined as the original Klon
Can be noisy at high gain

name: JHS Morning Glory V4
description: Transparent overdrive inspired by the Marshall Bluesbreaker circuit. Touch-sensitive and dynamic, it preserves your guitar's natural voice.

name: Boss OD-3 OverDrive
description: Dual-stage overdrive circuit for rich, natural breakup. More gain on tap than the SD-1 while keeping that classic Boss reliability.

name: Behringer TO800 Vintage Tube Overdrive
description: Budget Tube Screamer clone that punches way above its price. Same JRC4558 chip as the original TS808.

name: MXR Distortion+
description: One of the first distortion pedals ever made. Simple two-knob design beloved by Randy Rhoads and Jerry Garcia.

name: Friedman BE-OD
description: Captures the legendary Friedman BE-100 amp tone in a pedal. From classic crunch to searing high-gain lead tones.
cons: Can be harsh at extreme settings

name: TC Electronic Dark Matter
description: Versatile distortion with a voice switch for extra tonal range. From warm crunch to aggressive high-gain.

name: EarthQuaker Devices Hizumitas
description: Designed with Wata of Boris. Massive, crushing fuzz with a surprisingly musical tone control.

name: Way Huge Swollen Pickle MkIIS
description: Super versatile fuzz with extensive tone-shaping controls. From smooth sustain to sputtery chaos.
cons: Can be overwhelming to dial in

name: Strymon Timeline
description: The gold standard of delay pedals. 12 studio-class delay machines with MIDI, presets, and pristine audio quality.

name: TC Electronic Flashback 2
description: Versatile delay with TonePrint and MASH pressure-sensitive footswitch for expressive control.

name: Electro-Harmonix Canyon
description: 11 delay modes plus a 62-second looper in a compact pedal. From tape echo to shimmer.

name: MXR Dyna Comp
description: Classic Ross-style compressor. Simple two-knob design that adds sustain and punch to your tone.

name: Wampler Ego Compressor
description: Studio-quality compression with a blend knob for parallel compression. Transparent and musical.

name: TC Electronic Corona Chorus
description: Versatile chorus with TonePrint for custom tones. From subtle shimmer to deep, watery modulation.

name: Electro-Harmonix 720 Stereo Looper
description: 10 minutes of recording time with 12 memory slots. Unlimited overdubs and reverse playback.

name: Boss RC-1 Loop Station
description: Simple one-button looper with a unique circular LED indicator showing loop position. 12 minutes of recording.

name: TC Electronic PolyTune 3
description: Polyphonic tuning — strum all strings at once to see which are out. Built-in BonaFide buffer.

name: Korg Pitchblack Advance
description: Ultra-bright display visible in any lighting condition. Four display modes and ±0.1 cent accuracy.

name: Boss RV-6 Reverb
description: 8 reverb modes from subtle room to massive shimmer. The go-to reverb pedal for working musicians.

name: Electro-Harmonix Holy Grail Neo
description: Three classic reverb types: spring, hall, and plate. Simple one-knob design with lush, natural tones.

name: Strymon BigSky
description: 12 studio-class reverb machines. The ultimate reverb pedal for ambient, worship, and studio work.

name: TC Electronic Hall of Fame 2
description: Versatile reverb with MASH footswitch for expressive control and TonePrint for custom reverb algorithms.

name: Dunlop Cry Baby GCB95
description: The original wah pedal. Used by more guitarists than any other wah. Iconic vocal sweep.

name: Dunlop Cry Baby Mini
description: Half the size of the original with three voicing options. Perfect for tight pedalboards.

name: Vox V847-A Wah
description: Vintage Italian-style wah with warm, vocal sweep. The choice for classic rock and blues players.

name: Electro-Harmonix POG2
description: Polyphonic octave generator with incredible tracking. Sub octave, octave up, detune, and attack control.

name: MXR Sub Machine
description: Octave fuzz with sub-octave for massive low-end. Two voice modes for different fuzz characters.

name: Ernie Ball VP Jr 250K
description: Industry standard volume pedal. Smooth taper with tuner output for silent tuning.

name: Boss FV-500H Volume Pedal
description: Heavy-duty volume pedal with adjustable minimum volume. Built like a tank for touring.

name: Line 6 HX Stomp
description: Over 300 effects and amp models from the Helix family in a compact pedal. The Swiss Army knife of guitar effects.

name: Boss ME-80
description: Intuitive knob-per-function layout with COSM amp models. Easy to use without menu diving.

name: Boss DD-7 Digital Delay
description: The Boss DD-7 is the most versatile delay in the classic Boss lineup. With 11 delay modes including analog, reverse, modulate, and a dedicated looper function, it is the workhorse delay pedal for gigging musicians.

name: JHS Bender
description: The JHS Bender is a faithful recreation of the iconic Tone Bender MKI.5 fuzz pedal made famous by Jimmy Page and Jeff Beck. Germanium transistors give it a warm, dynamic fuzz with incredible touch response.

name: Electro-Harmonix Small Stone
description: The Small Stone is a classic analog phaser with a rich, swirling tone that has appeared on countless records since the 1970s. Its simple Rate knob and Color switch make it easy to dial in from subtle to dramatic phase.

name: Electro-Harmonix Pulsar
description: The EHX Pulsar is a full-featured analog tremolo with four wave shapes — triangle, sine, square, and sawtooth — plus a width control for precise depth adjustment. Its tap tempo footswitch keeps you locked to the beat.

name: MXR M108S 10-Band EQ
description: The MXR 10-Band EQ gives you surgical frequency control with 10 sliders covering the full guitar frequency range. Built-in LED clip indicator and output volume make it easy to set up as a boost or tone-shaping tool.

name: Electro-Harmonix Wailer Wah
description: The EHX Wailer Wah features an adjustable Q control to shape the width of your wah sweep from narrow and sharp to wide and smooth. Auto-engage switch and voice controls let you tailor the classic wah sound to your style.

name: Boss OC-5 Octave
description: The Boss OC-5 delivers the classic OC-2 analog sound alongside a new polyphonic mode for chords and arpeggios. From sub-bass rumble to upper-octave lead tones, it covers all the octave bases.
pros: Range control for guitar or bass

name: Boss ME-90 Multi-Effects
description: The Boss ME-90 brings the power of the GT series in a knob-per-function floor unit. 200 preset patches, AIRD amp models, and a built-in looper make it the complete package for gigging guitarists.

name: Line 6 POD Go
description: The Line 6 POD Go delivers Helix-quality amp modeling and effects in a lightweight, tour-ready floor unit. 270+ amps, cabs, and effects with an intuitive interface and built-in expression pedal.
